details.reason: golden has no quoted_span — run draft_quote_spans.py first

samples.claim: According to the Council on Integrity in Results Reporting (CIRR), the median bootcamp tuition in 2024 was $16,500 (CIRR).

Some bootcamps offer Income Share Agreements (ISAs), where students pay a percentage of their salary after employment, though these are becoming less common due to regulatory scrutiny (Course Report).
In-state public universities average $40,000–$60,000 for tuition alone, while private institutions can exceed $200,000 (College Board).
The opportunity cost of four years of foregone wages is substantial—estimated at $120,000–$200,000 in lost income (Bureau of Labor Statistics).
freeCodeCamp offers a complete curriculum at no cost (freeCodeCamp).
Coursera's subscription costs $49–$79/month, and a typical 12-month self-study plan totals $0–$500 (Coursera).
Multiverse pays apprentices a salary during training ($20,000–$40,000 annually) (Multiverse).
Microverse uses an ISA model where graduates pay 15% of their salary for 24 months after reaching a $50,000 income threshold (Microverse).

**P1: 3–6 Months Post-Graduation**
CIRR-audited data shows that 71% of bootcamp graduates find a job within 180 days of graduation, with a median job search of 4.2 months (CIRR).
However, graduates typically secure jobs within 1–3 months of graduation, with placement rates exceeding 90% at top programs (NACE).
A survey of 1,200 self-taught developers found a median of 18 months from starting to first job, with 25% taking over 2 years (Stack Overflow Developer Survey).
Multiverse reports an average program duration of 12 months with 90% of apprentices converting to full-time roles (Multiverse Outcomes).
In 2024, CIRR-audited bootcamps reported a 71% placement rate within 180 days, rising to 79% within one year (CIRR).
Non-audited bootcamps often claim 85–95% placement, but these figures are less reliable (Course Report).

**P2: 85–95%**
The National Association of Colleges and Employers (NACE) reports that 87% of computer science graduates secure employment within six months of graduation, with top-tier programs exceed
Surveys indicate that 30–50% of self-taught individuals who actively seek employment find a software engineering role within two years (freeCodeCamp Survey).
Multiverse claims 90% placement within 6 months of program completion (Multiverse).
Microverse reports 82% placement within 12 months (Microverse).

**P1: $70,000–$95,000**
CIRR data shows median first-year salaries for bootcamp graduates at $82,000 in 2024, with top performers earning over $100,000 (CIRR).
The National Association of Colleges and Employers reports a median starting salary of $95,000 for computer science graduates in 2024, with top programs averaging $115,000 (NACE).

verdicts: Are exactly 4 paths defined (bootcamp / CS degree / self-taught / apprenticeship)?
Are exactly 5 dimensions enumerated (cost / time / placement / salary / hireability)?
Is a 4 x 5 matrix presented with all 20 cells populated?
Does each cell cite >= 1 shopping + >= 1 reddit + >= 1 wiki URL?
Are total-cost ranges cited for each path with $ amounts?
Is median time-to-first-job (months) explicitly stated per path?
Are placement-rate numbers cited (with CIRR audit reference if applicable)?
Are first-year salary numbers cited with reddit or wiki source?
Is FAANG-hireability discussed per path with reddit evidence?
Are >= 10 shopping product pages enumerated with price + rating?
Are >= 30 reddit threads from at least 4 CS-career sub-forums cited?
Are all 10 mandatory wiki articles cited?
Are 4 user-persona paragraphs included (one per path)?
Does each persona paragraph cite >= 5 reddit threads?
Are at least 3 cross-source contradictions surfaced (marketing claim vs reddit reality)?
Are all cited URLs markdown-linked and sandbox-resolvable?
Is per-domain minimum met: >= 10 shopping, >= 30 reddit, >= 25 wiki?
Does the report avoid TOP-N format and chain-of-thought?
